subject: fix(debug): LogRow renders event + details through redactUrls (security/SEC1)
body:
The Env tab redacts env.url at construction and debugGenerateReport
runs a final redactUrls sweep before the clipboard copy — so Env-tab
screenshots and pasted reports are both screenshot-safe. But the
Log tab's on-screen rendering of entry.event and entry.details did
NOT redact. A screenshot of the open pill's Log tab could leak URLs
that the report-copy path would redact.

Concrete leak:
1. App does console.error('Failed:', new Error('POST https://api/?apikey=X ...'))
   or debugAdd('supabase', 'error', 'Request failed',
     { url: 'https://api.com/?apikey=SECRET' })
2. Entry lands in buffer; event + details contain the URL.
3. User opens DebugPill → Log tab. URL + apikey visible on screen.
4. Screenshot → leaked.

Fix: apply redactUrls in LogRow at render time to both
  - entry.event (string)
  - the stringified entry.details (whether details is already a
    string or an object that needs safeStringify)

Matches the screenshot-safety pattern already applied to env.url.
The raw entry.details are still unmodified in the in-memory buffer
(preserves programmatic access for any future debug tooling); only
the displayed strings go through redaction.

New test covers the exact chain LogRow uses:
  redactUrls(typeof details === 'string' ? details : safeStringify(details))
Three assertions: structured detail with URL, string detail with URL,
event string with URL. All three paths must not leak the query.

Other security findings from the sweep:
- window.__debugPushError / __debugClearLoadTimer exposed globals:
  low-risk, XSS-safe by design (textContent, not innerHTML).
- 'unsafe-inline' CSP for scripts: pre-existing tradeoff, not touched
  by this branch.
- Supabase anon key + Mapbox token: intentionally public per each
  platform's security model (RLS server-side; URL-restricted token).
- localStorage writes: timestamps + browser name, no PII.
- Linking.openURL / window.open: hardcoded URLs only, no user-input
  redirect surface.
- eval / new Function: none.
- Exposed secrets in branch code: none.

tsc 0, jest 92/92 (up from 91 — one new `it` block with three asserts),
expo web build clean.

Tags: debug, security, privacy, redaction
Complexity: 1
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: debug-pill-hardening
Semver: patch
